summary: Tested all 6 requested features for CineWorld Studio improvements. All features working correctly: 20 modern avatars (DiceBear v9), mini-game translations (IT, EN, ES, FR, DE), 16 genres with 6 sub-genres each (max 3 selectable), 5 actor roles with translations.

issues: Missing DialogDescription warning in console (accessibility) - minor

passed_tests: Avatars API: Returns exactly 20 avatars (7 male, 7 female, 6 fantasy)
Avatars: All use DiceBear v9 API with modern styles (notionists, lorelei, personas, thumbs, bottts-neutral, glass)
Genres API: Returns exactly 16 genres (action, comedy, drama, horror, sci_fi, romance, thriller, animation, documentary, fantasy, musical, western, war, noir, adventure, biographical)
Sub-genres: Each genre has exactly 6 sub-genres
Sub-genres UI: Film wizard shows sub-genres with 'max 3' limit indicator
Sub-genres selection: Can select/deselect sub-genres with visual badges
Actor Roles API: Returns 5 roles (protagonist, co_protagonist, antagonist, supporting, cameo)
Actor Roles Translations: All 5 roles have translations for IT, ES, FR, DE
Actor Roles UI: Role dropdown appears after selecting actor in wizard step 6
Actor Roles Display: Selected actors show role in badge (e.g., 'Thomas Brown (Protagonist)')
Mini-games Italian: Trivia questions appear in Italian for IT-language user
Mini-games Italian: Genre game options use Italian genre names
Mini-games Italian: Director match questions in Italian ('Chi ha diretto...')
Mini-games: All 5 games available (Film Trivia, Guess the Genre, Director Match, Box Office Bet, Release Year)
Film Wizard: 10-step wizard works with new genre/subgenre/role features
Language Selector: 5 languages available (EN, IT, ES, FR, DE)

20_modern_avatars.details: 20 avatars using DiceBear v9 - styles: notionists, lorelei, personas, thumbs, bottts-neutral, glass. Categories: 7 male, 7 female, 6 fantasy

mini_game_translations.details: Questions translated in 5 languages. Tested IT user - questions appear in Italian (e.g., 'Chi ha interpretato il Joker in Il cavaliere oscuro?')

16_genres_with_subgenres.details: 16 genres: action, comedy, drama, horror, sci_fi, romance, thriller, animation, documentary, fantasy, musical, western, war, noir, adventure, biographical. Each has 6 sub-genres

subgenre_selection_max_3.details: UI shows 'Sub-genres (max 3)' label. Can select/deselect sub-genres as badges. Visual feedback shows 'Selected: X, Y'

actor_roles_endpoint.details: GET /api/actor-roles returns 5 roles: protagonist, co_protagonist, antagonist, supporting, cameo with translations

actor_role_selection_in_wizard.details: Step 6 of Film Wizard shows role dropdown after selecting actor. Default role is 'Protagonist'. Selected actors display as 'Name (Role)' badge

context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 requested features verified working: 1) 20 avatars with DiceBear v9 modern styles, 2) Mini-games translated in 5 languages (tested IT), 3) 16 genres with 6 sub-genres each, 4) Sub-genre selection limited to max 3, 5) Actor roles endpoint with 5 roles and translations, 6) Actor role selection in film wizard step 6. Demo user has IT language set, useful for testing translations.
